prompt:
Create a single-frame, photorealistic editorial image that physically incarnates the machine learning concept of overfitting.

Scene: a believable locksmith workshop photographed like a high-end magazine feature. In the foreground, on a scarred wooden bench, sits one old brass padlock with visible dents, scratches, burrs, and accumulated grime inside the keyway — a lock damaged in many tiny, highly specific ways. Inserted into it is an absurdly intricate brass key whose teeth are grotesquely over-carved with excessive micro-notches, spikes, and tiny irregular cuts, clearly tailored to every flaw of this one damaged lock.

Nearby, in the same frame, place several clean, nearly identical new padlocks from the same manufacturer. They remain unopened and untouched, implying that the bizarre key is too narrowly specialized to work for the general case. The relationship between the one damaged lock and the many normal locks must be instantly readable.

The image should make the viewer feel that something has been optimized too perfectly for one flawed example and has therefore become brittle, narrow, and useless elsewhere.

Style: physically plausible, cinematic but realistic, premium editorial photography, warm workshop light, rich brass and wood textures, shallow depth of field, restrained palette, no fantasy elements.

Composition: single frame only, no split panels. The damaged lock and over-detailed key dominate the foreground in sharp focus. The clean locks sit in softer but still legible focus behind and beside it, so the failure to generalize is visible without explanation.

Hard constraints: no text, no labels, no equations, no diagrams, no charts, no floating symbols, no glowing AI effects, no holograms, no code, no abstract data streams. Everything must look like a real photographed scene.

Output: visually striking, structurally precise, expert-recognizable as overfitting, exhibition-quality.
metaphor: An excessively carved key fits one damaged lock's exact defects, but fails the many clean locks beside it.
